title: orthocarpus laciniatus
intro: Orthocarpus laciniatus, also known as the mountain lupine, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat various kidney and urinary issues. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Orthocarpus laciniatus can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in tinctures and capsules, making it a versatile herb for natural remedies.
